name: NIST AI Risk Management Framework
description: The NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF) provides guidance for organizations to manage risks associated with AI systems throughout their lifecycle. It focuses on trustworthy AI characteristics: valid, reliable, safe, secure, resilient, accountable, transparent, explainable, interpretable, privacy-enhanced, and fair.
organization: National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST)

categories.name: Govern
categories.description: Cultivate and implement a culture of risk management within organizations designing, developing, deploying, evaluating, or acquiring AI systems.

controls.name: AI Risk Management Strategy
controls.description: Establish organizational AI risk management policies, processes, procedures, and practices.

requirements.description: Legal and regulatory requirements involving AI are understood, managed, and documented.
requirements.guidance: Review AI governance policies, regulatory compliance mapping, and legal review processes.

requirements.description: AI risk tolerance is determined and documented for the organization.
requirements.guidance: Examine AI risk tolerance statements and risk appetite documentation.

requirements.description: Processes, procedures, and practices are in place to determine the needed level of risk management activities.
requirements.guidance: Review AI risk tiering methodology and assessment procedures.

requirements.description: Ongoing monitoring and periodic review of the risk management process and outcomes are planned and organizational roles and responsibilities established.
requirements.guidance: Examine AI governance committee structure, roles, and monitoring cadence.

requirements.description: Organizational policies and practices are in place to foster a critical thinking culture regarding AI risk.
requirements.guidance: Review AI ethics training, escalation procedures, and dissent mechanisms.

requirements.description: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and track risk decisions.
requirements.guidance: Examine AI system inventory, model registry, and risk decision logs.

requirements.description: Processes and procedures are in place for decommissioning AI systems safely.
requirements.guidance: Review AI decommissioning policies and data retention procedures.

controls.name: Accountability Structures
controls.description: Establish accountability structures, roles, and responsibilities for AI risk management.

requirements.description: Roles and responsibilities for AI risk management are defined and understood.
requirements.guidance: Review RACI matrix, job descriptions, and organizational charts for AI governance.

requirements.description: Mechanisms are in place to ensure accountability for AI system impacts.
requirements.guidance: Examine incident response procedures, audit trails, and escalation paths.

requirements.description: Executive leadership takes responsibility for decisions about AI system risks.
requirements.guidance: Review executive sign-off procedures and risk acceptance documentation.

controls.name: AI Workforce Diversity
controls.description: Build diverse and inclusive AI teams to enhance AI system trustworthiness.

requirements.description: Decision-making related to mapping, measuring, and managing AI risks involves diverse perspectives and teams.
requirements.guidance: Review team composition, cross-functional review processes, and diversity metrics.

requirements.description: Policies and procedures address skill gaps related to AI development and deployment.
requirements.guidance: Examine training programs, hiring practices, and competency frameworks.

controls.name: Organizational Context
controls.description: Understand the organizational context including mission, goals, and values relative to AI risk management.

requirements.description: Organizational policies and practices align AI system objectives with human values and societal benefits.
requirements.guidance: Review AI ethics policies, value statements, and societal impact assessments.

requirements.description: Organizational teams document AI system context including intended purpose, domain, and deployment environment.
requirements.guidance: Examine model cards, system documentation, and use case definitions.

requirements.description: Organizational policies address procurement and use of third-party AI systems.
requirements.guidance: Review vendor assessment procedures and third-party AI due diligence processes.

controls.name: Third-Party AI Risk
controls.description: Manage AI risks related to third-party entities throughout the AI lifecycle.

requirements.description: Policies and procedures are in place for assessing third-party AI systems and data.
requirements.guidance: Review vendor questionnaires, assessment frameworks, and due diligence checklists.

requirements.description: Contractual mechanisms address AI-specific risks with third parties.
requirements.guidance: Examine contract templates, SLAs, and AI-specific clauses.

controls.name: Stakeholder Engagement
controls.description: Engage with relevant stakeholders including affected communities in AI risk management.

requirements.description: Policies and procedures are in place for engaging with AI system stakeholders.
requirements.guidance: Review stakeholder engagement plans, feedback mechanisms, and community involvement.

requirements.description: Mechanisms exist for stakeholders to provide input on AI system development and deployment.
requirements.guidance: Examine feedback channels, public comment processes, and user testing procedures.

categories.name: Map
categories.description: Identify and categorize AI system context, capabilities, purposes, and potential impacts to inform risk management activities.

controls.name: Context and Use Case
controls.description: Establish the context and intended use of AI systems.

requirements.description: The intended purposes, potentially beneficial uses, context of use, and design specifications are documented.
requirements.guidance: Review system design documents, use case specifications, and requirements.

requirements.description: Potential positive and negative impacts of AI systems are identified and documented.
requirements.guidance: Examine impact assessments, risk registers, and benefit-risk analyses.

requirements.description: AI system functions, capabilities, and limitations are clearly documented.
requirements.guidance: Review technical specifications, model cards, and capability statements.

requirements.description: AI system goals, objectives, and constraints are defined and documented.
requirements.guidance: Examine requirements documents, design rationale, and constraint specifications.

requirements.description: The AI system is mapped to relevant AI Actor Tasks and AI lifecycle phases.
requirements.guidance: Review lifecycle documentation and actor responsibility matrices.

requirements.description: Scientific integrity and data quality standards are identified and documented.
requirements.guidance: Examine data quality policies, validation procedures, and methodology documentation.

controls.name: AI Impact Categorization
controls.description: Categorize AI systems based on potential impacts to inform risk management priorities.

requirements.description: AI system risks are identified and assessed based on potential impacts to individuals, communities, and society.
requirements.guidance: Review impact categorization methodology and risk tiering criteria.

requirements.description: Potential harms and unintended consequences of AI systems are identified and documented.
requirements.guidance: Examine harm taxonomies, consequence analyses, and risk scenarios.

requirements.description: AI system risk categorization considers frequency, severity, and reversibility of potential impacts.
requirements.guidance: Review risk scoring methodology and impact assessment criteria.

controls.name: Benefits and Costs
controls.description: Evaluate and document AI system benefits and costs to enable informed decision-making.

requirements.description: Potential benefits of the AI system are documented and weighed against potential risks.
requirements.guidance: Review benefit-cost analyses, value propositions, and risk-benefit trade-offs.

requirements.description: Decisions about AI system deployment consider effects on different populations.
requirements.guidance: Examine equity assessments and disparate impact analyses.

controls.name: Risks and Benefits over Time
controls.description: Monitor and manage evolving AI system risks and benefits throughout the lifecycle.

requirements.description: Processes are in place to continually assess AI system risks and benefits.
requirements.guidance: Review ongoing monitoring plans, reassessment schedules, and change management procedures.

requirements.description: Changes in the deployment environment or use context trigger reassessment of AI system risks.
requirements.guidance: Examine change triggers, reassessment criteria, and adaptation procedures.

controls.name: AI Actor Risks
controls.description: Identify and manage risks associated with AI actors throughout the AI lifecycle.

requirements.description: Potential negative impacts from AI actors are identified and documented.
requirements.guidance: Review actor risk assessments, insider threat considerations, and access controls.

requirements.description: Practices are in place to evaluate trustworthiness of AI actors.
requirements.guidance: Examine background check procedures, training requirements, and certification processes.

categories.name: Measure
categories.description: Analyze, assess, benchmark, and monitor AI risks and impacts, based on the AI system's context and potential impacts.

controls.name: AI Risk Assessment
controls.description: Develop and apply methodologies for measuring AI-specific risks.

requirements.description: Approaches and metrics for measuring AI system trustworthiness characteristics are identified and implemented.
requirements.guidance: Review trustworthiness metrics, measurement methodologies, and evaluation criteria.

requirements.description: Appropriate methods and metrics are used to evaluate AI system performance and safety.
requirements.guidance: Examine performance testing procedures, safety evaluations, and validation methods.

requirements.description: AI system testing and evaluation results are documented and maintained.
requirements.guidance: Review test documentation, evaluation reports, and results archives.

controls.name: Trustworthiness Evaluation
controls.description: Evaluate AI systems for trustworthiness characteristics including reliability, validity, and safety.

requirements.description: AI systems are tested for validity and reliability under expected operating conditions.
requirements.guidance: Review validation testing, reliability assessments, and robustness evaluations.

requirements.description: AI systems are evaluated for safety including fail-safe behaviors.
requirements.guidance: Examine safety testing, failure mode analysis, and contingency procedures.

requirements.description: AI systems are tested for security and resilience against attacks.
requirements.guidance: Review adversarial testing, security assessments, and resilience evaluations.

requirements.description: AI systems are evaluated for privacy risks and data protection.
requirements.guidance: Examine privacy impact assessments, data protection evaluations, and anonymization testing.

requirements.description: AI systems are evaluated for explainability and transparency.
requirements.guidance: Review explainability testing, transparency assessments, and interpretability evaluations.

requirements.description: AI systems are evaluated for fairness and bias.
requirements.guidance: Examine bias testing, fairness metrics, and disparate impact analyses.

requirements.description: AI system training data is evaluated for representativeness and quality.
requirements.guidance: Review data quality assessments, representativeness analyses, and data provenance.

requirements.description: AI models are evaluated for drift and degradation over time.
requirements.guidance: Examine model monitoring, drift detection, and performance degradation tracking.

requirements.description: Human oversight mechanisms are tested and evaluated.
requirements.guidance: Review human-in-the-loop testing, override capabilities, and escalation procedures.

requirements.description: Environmental impacts of AI systems are considered and evaluated.
requirements.guidance: Examine carbon footprint assessments, resource utilization, and sustainability metrics.

requirements.description: Safety metrics are defined and tracked for AI systems with safety implications.
requirements.guidance: Review safety KPIs, incident metrics, and harm prevention measures.

requirements.description: AI systems are evaluated in real-world or representative environments.
requirements.guidance: Examine field testing, pilot deployments, and real-world evaluation procedures.

requirements.description: Effectiveness of risk mitigation measures is evaluated.
requirements.guidance: Review mitigation effectiveness testing and control validation.

controls.name: Feedback and Monitoring
controls.description: Establish mechanisms for ongoing monitoring, feedback, and assessment of AI systems.

requirements.description: Feedback mechanisms are established for AI system stakeholders to report issues.
requirements.guidance: Review feedback channels, issue reporting procedures, and stakeholder communication.

requirements.description: AI system performance is continuously monitored in deployment.
requirements.guidance: Examine monitoring dashboards, alerting mechanisms, and performance tracking.

requirements.description: AI system incidents and near-misses are tracked and analyzed.
requirements.guidance: Review incident tracking systems, root cause analysis procedures, and lessons learned.

controls.name: Independent Evaluation
controls.description: Conduct independent evaluations and audits of AI systems.

requirements.description: AI systems undergo independent evaluation or audit as appropriate.
requirements.guidance: Review third-party audit reports, independent assessments, and external evaluations.

requirements.description: Results of independent evaluations are documented and addressed.
requirements.guidance: Examine audit findings, remediation plans, and follow-up actions.

requirements.description: Processes are in place to share measurement results with relevant stakeholders.
requirements.guidance: Review reporting procedures, stakeholder communication, and transparency mechanisms.

categories.name: Manage
categories.description: Prioritize and respond to AI risks through risk treatment plans, resource allocation, and monitoring.

controls.name: Risk Prioritization
controls.description: Prioritize AI risks to allocate resources effectively.

requirements.description: AI system risks are prioritized based on assessed likelihood and impact.
requirements.guidance: Review risk prioritization methodology, scoring criteria, and ranking procedures.

requirements.description: Risk prioritization considers context-specific factors and stakeholder input.
requirements.guidance: Examine stakeholder input processes, contextual factors, and prioritization rationale.

requirements.description: Risk prioritization decisions and rationale are documented.
requirements.guidance: Review decision documentation, risk registers, and prioritization records.

controls.name: Risk Treatment
controls.description: Develop and implement risk treatment plans for identified AI risks.

requirements.description: Risk treatment plans are developed for prioritized AI system risks.
requirements.guidance: Review risk treatment strategies, mitigation plans, and control implementations.

requirements.description: Mechanisms are in place to track risk treatment implementation and effectiveness.
requirements.guidance: Examine treatment tracking, effectiveness monitoring, and remediation status.

requirements.description: Post-deployment AI system monitoring plans are developed and implemented.
requirements.guidance: Review monitoring strategies, alerting mechanisms, and ongoing assessment procedures.

requirements.description: Risk treatment decisions are documented and communicated to stakeholders.
requirements.guidance: Examine decision documentation, stakeholder communication, and transparency measures.

controls.name: Resource Allocation
controls.description: Allocate resources commensurate with AI risk levels and treatment needs.

requirements.description: Resources are allocated for AI risk management activities based on risk levels.
requirements.guidance: Review resource allocation decisions, budget documentation, and staffing plans.

requirements.description: AI risk management teams have appropriate expertise and authority.
requirements.guidance: Examine team composition, expertise assessments, and authority documentation.

controls.name: Residual Risk
controls.description: Document and communicate residual risks after treatment implementation.

requirements.description: Residual AI system risks are documented and communicated to decision-makers.
requirements.guidance: Review residual risk documentation, acceptance decisions, and communication records.

requirements.description: Plans are in place to monitor and manage residual risks over time.
requirements.guidance: Examine ongoing monitoring plans, reassessment schedules, and residual risk tracking.
